channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Congress MLA Accuses LDF Government of Delaying Spillway Opening During 2018 Kerala Floods
item.description: On Tuesday, Congress MLA Mathew Kuzhalnadan from Muvattupuzha, Ernakulam district, accused the LDF government of corruption linked to the 2018 Kerala floods. Speaking at a press conference, he release...

item.title: High Oil Prices Could Squeeze Housing Market and Consumer Budgets in South Africa
item.description: A recent report from Professor Simphiwe Madikizela, an economist at the University of South Africa, has highlighted the broader economic impact of rising global oil prices on the South African residen...

item.title: Lesedi Matsunyane-Ferguson discusses her role in the South African comedy 'Destroying Desmond'
item.description: Lesedi Matsunyane-Ferguson, the actress known for her famous parents Connie Ferguson and Neo Matsunyane, recently discussed her role in the chaotic South African comedy film 'Destroying Desmond'. The ...

item.title: Woman wrongly arrested after reporting harassment by ex-partner
item.description: Jodie Morrow, a student at Ulster University, experienced harassment from her ex-boyfriend, Ezra Garfield, after ending their brief relationship in 2020. Despite repeatedly reporting his threatening m...

item.title: Manny Pacquiao throws ceremonial first pitch at New York Yankees game
item.description: Boxing legend and former eight-division world champion Manny Pacquiao added another memorable moment to his ongoing US visit by throwing the ceremonial first pitch at Yankee Stadium in New York. The e...

item.title: Santa Fe Resident Dies After Snakebite
item.description: A man in Santa Fe, Romblon, tragically died after being bitten by a snake. The incident occurred recently, leaving the community mourning the loss. Local authorities are investigating the circumstance...

item.title: ODM's Current Struggles Linked to Broader Political Alliances
item.description: The ongoing confusion within the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) party has been blamed on the consequences of political alliances made during the broad-based deal, according to Taabu Tele. The ODM is...

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs Launch Women’s Team with Focus on Grassroots Promotion
item.description: After much anticipation, Kaizer Chiefs have officially launched their women’s football team, marking a significant step in the growth of women’s football in South Africa. Unlike previous efforts where...

item.title: Saskatchewan residents who fought wildfire face challenges in recovering $39K firefighting costs
item.description: In Wadin Bay, Saskatchewan, residents took matters into their own hands to protect their community from the Pisew wildfire in 2025. Despite a mandatory evacuation order, nearly two dozen volunteers st...

item.title: Rigathi Gachagua Names Vincent Kibet Rotich as DCP Nominee for Emurua Dikirr By-Election
item.description: Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua, who also leads the Democracy for Citizens Party (DCP), has officially presented Vincent Kibet Rotich as the party’s candidate for the Emurua Dikirr parliamentary by-...

item.title: Angus Anderson to debut for Collingwood as Nick Daicos and Scott Pendlebury cleared; Hinkley criticises AFL fine
item.description: Collingwood is set to introduce mature-aged recruit Angus Anderson to their AFL lineup, following a tough loss to the Lions. Anderson, who was drafted at No.57 last year and has experience in the SANF...

item.title: Uttarakhand Police Struggles to Enforce Uniform Civil Code Due to Technical Delay
item.description: Despite the implementation of the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in Uttarakhand in January 2025, the state police are yet to register cases under its provisions due to a delay in updating the Crime and Crim...

item.title: Silvertip Resort gondola proposal faces resident opposition in Canmore
item.description: Silvertip Resort, located near Canmore, Alberta, has proposed the development of a new gondola system as part of its expansion plans. The project aims to enhance tourism and provide better access to t...

item.title: South Africa Receives First Shipment of Anti-HIV Injection LEN, Awaiting Quality Testing
item.description: South Africa has received its first consignment of lenacapavir (LEN), a twice-yearly anti-HIV injection, with 37,920 doses landing at OR Tambo International Airport in late March and early April, six ...

item.title: U.S. and Iran establish temporary two-week ceasefire amid escalating tensions
item.description: The United States and Iran have agreed to a temporary two-week ceasefire, following a period of heightened tensions and stern warnings from President Donald Trump. The agreement comes amid concerns ab...

item.title: Heavy Rainstorm Damages Kugbo Bus Terminal in Abuja, Causes Traffic Chaos
item.description: A heavy rainstorm with strong winds yesterday afternoon around 3:30pm wreaked serious havoc on the multimillion naira Kugbo Bus Terminal along the Abuja-Keffi highway in the Federal Capital Territory....

item.title: Exploring Nostalgia and Nature in Anton Van Hertbruggen’s Art
item.description: Belgian artist Anton Van Hertbruggen is celebrated for his striking paintings that blend nostalgia, nature, and imagination in a seamless harmony. His newest collection, featured on Moss and Fog, capt...

item.title: The ethical dilemma of denying children life-saving treatment for religious reasons
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal’s health MEC, Nomagugu Simelane, recently condemned a local religious group, iKhaya Labafundi, for discouraging its members from seeking life-saving medical treatment, including antiretr...

item.title: The Jewish Labour Bund: A Radical Socialist Movement in Europe
item.description: Molly Crabapple’s new book, 'Here Where We Live Is Our Country: The Story of the Jewish Bund', chronicles the history of the Jewish Labour Bund, a socialist movement founded in 1897 in the Tsarist emp...

item.title: West Bengal's Voter List Faces Major Shrinkage, 27 Lakh Excluded Ahead of Assembly Elections
item.description: West Bengal’s Special Intensive Revision (SIR) has led to a significant reduction in its electorate. As of April 7, 2026, 27 lakh voters have been excluded after judicial scrutiny, resulting in a 12% ...
